llava_med.response: Axial CT shows linear opacities and focal volume loss in the posterior segments. The morphology is consistent with subsegmental atelectasis rather than an active inflammatory process.

medgemma.findings: Peripheral, subpleural ground-glass opacities (GGO) are noted bilaterally. No significant pleural effusion or lymphadenopathy identified. Bronchovascular bundling is slightly prominent within the opacified areas.
medgemma.impression: Findings are highly suggestive of viral pneumonia, consistent with the provided clinical context of COVID-19.

llava_med.response: The image demonstrates wedge-shaped areas of increased density with associated shifting of the interlobar fissures, suggestive of compressive atelectasis.

medgemma.impression: Findings consistent with multifocal bacterial pneumonia.

llava_med.response: Computed tomography of the chest shows focal ground-glass opacities with a predominantly peripheral and posterior distribution. No significant pleural effusion is noted.

medgemma.findings: Small, ill-defined subpleural ground-glass nodules in the left upper and right lower lobes. The remainder of the lung parenchyma is clear. Heart size is within normal limits.
medgemma.impression: Atypical pulmonary opacities likely representing mild or early-stage COVID-19 pneumonia.

llava_med.response: The axial slice at the level of the main pulmonary artery shows bilateral peripheral infiltrates. The morphology of the opacities is consistent with clinical patterns observed in COVID-19 infection.

medgemma.impression: Radiographic features suggestive of acute pulmonary edema.

medgemma.impression: Advanced infectious process consistent with necrotizing pneumonia.
